purposes.description: Conduct statistical hypothesis tests (t-test, paired t-test, ANOVA, chi-square, Pearson correlation) with proper assumption checking (normality, homogeneity of variance) and multiple comparison correction.

primitives.evidence: Requires scipy.stats (third-party) for stats.ttest_ind, stats.f_oneway, stats.chi2_contingency, stats.pearsonr, stats.shapiro, stats.levene. Also statsmodels for power analysis.
Agent must select the correct test based on the test selection guide: 2 groups -> t-test, 3+ groups -> ANOVA, categorical -> chi-square, continuous relationships -> correlation/regression.
Interpreting p-values, computing Bonferroni correction (p_adjusted = min(p_raw * n_tests, 1.0)), and understanding significance thresholds.

purposes.description: Build and interpret multiple linear regression models using OLS, including coefficient significance, R-squared, VIF for multicollinearity detection.

primitives.evidence: Uses statsmodels.api (third-party): sm.OLS, sm.add_constant, model.summary(), model.rsquared, model.pvalues. Also statsmodels.stats.outliers_influence.variance_inflation_factor.
Interpreting regression output: R-squared, adjusted R-squared, F-statistic, individual coefficient p-values, and VIF > 10 threshold for multicollinearity.

purposes.description: Calculate and interpret effect sizes (Cohen's d, eta-squared, r) to quantify practical significance alongside statistical significance.

primitives.evidence: Computing Cohen's d with pooled standard deviation using numpy (third-party), eta-squared from sum-of-squares decomposition.
Multi-step calculations: pooled SD = sqrt(((n_a-1)*var_a + (n_b-1)*var_b) / (n_a+n_b-2)), then d = (mean_a - mean_b) / pooled_sd. SS_between/SS_total for eta-squared.

purposes.description: Format statistical results in APA style and as structured JSON output with descriptives, test statistics, p-values, and effect sizes.

primitives.evidence: JSON output format has nested structure: descriptives array, anova object, pairwise array with multiple fields per entry. ~1-2KB output.
APA reporting format has specific conventions: t(df) = X.XX, p = .XXX, d = X.XX. JSON schema has exact field names and types.
